svg.text: Two orthogonal axes of substrate-independence — body-layer (Soma) and runtime-layer (cortex/myelin IAW)
Design ref: docs/design-soma-integration.md · cortex#110 (Internet of Agentic Work) · 2026-05-17
one body, projected into many substrates · ~/.soma/profile/<assistant>/
↓ Soma adapter contract · projects body → substrate-native file shape ↓
↑ orange = substrate-native projections · same body, four file shapes · Soma adapter owns the projection (not the host)

g.text: JOIN KEY — one new schema field on policy.principals[]
ties NKey-stamped bus principal ↔ Soma assistant body · schema flip lands in Phase C.2b/c (PR #291)

svg.text: — body-layer above · runtime-layer below — the two axes compose through the join key above —
M1 – M7 Myelin layer model · the bus / transport / composition spine
cortex consumes M2–M6, owns no part of M1–M6, shares M7 with sibling apps (pilot, signal-collector, future). Soma is NOT an M-layer — it crosses them.

g.text: cortex (this app) · pilot · signal-collector · future M7 apps — all can host Soma assistants
surface-router · dispatch-handler · PolicyEngine · pipeline / fan-out / fan-in / request-reply (myelin)
capability registry · JetStream consumers · network-registry (Phase D cloud-side)
verifiable principal per envelope · subject routing local / federated / public · PrincipalRegistry
message format + sovereignty · chain-of-stamps signed_by[] · NKey signatures · (myelin · shipped)
abstract bus · pub/sub · request/reply · MyelinRuntime interface (NATS abstraction)
TCP / TLS / leaf-nodes / federation · NATS · cloud registry endpoints (Phase D)
where the substrate-independence work lands on the stack
M6 / M7 · dispatch into any LLM substrate via a common interface · done
M3 / M4 · 3-tier identity: operator → stack → agent · shipped
Phase C · PolicyEngine + policy.principals[] ◀ IN FLIGHT
M6 · PR #291 C.2b/c breaking schema flip · Soma join field rides here (C.5)
↳ one and only operator-facing schema flip in the IAW plan
M5–M7 · one assistant projects into N substrates · requires Soma

svg.text: join-key consumed by M6 PolicyEngine + M3 envelope chain-of-stamps

g.text: What cortex reinvents today → one Soma adapter contract
Three substrate adapters, three hand-rolled persona-projection shims, no common contract. Soma absorbs all three.
Federation across the operator boundary — the Soma-federated model (option a)
Andreas's Luna delegates to JC Fischer's sage. Both backed by Soma bodies; both signed under their own stack NKeys; both addressable across operators.
The load-bearing claim — two orthogonal axes of the same independence story
· SessionHarness (A) — dispatch into ANY LLM substrate via one interface
· PolicyEngine (C) — authorization lifted out of substrate-coupled adapters
· Federation (D/E) — stacks compose across operators regardless of substrate
↳ answers: how does the BUS dispatch + identify + authorize, substrate-neutrally?
· Identity, Telos, ISA, Skills, Memory, Policy — the assistant's portable body
· Projection contract — one body → CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules/persona.mdc, AGENTS.md, NATS sub
· Writeback gate — substrate sessions writing back to Soma source, gated by policy
· Daemon mode — Soma assistant subscribing to Myelin subjects directly, no substrate
↳ answers: how is the ASSISTANT itself portable, substrate-neutrally?
A Phase E delegation — "your main assistant delegates around and coordinates on your behalf" — is only coherent if your main
assistant exists independently of any one substrate. Soma gives the body. cortex/myelin gives the bus. Both are needed.

svg.text: Source: docs/design-soma-integration.md §1–§9 · the-metafactory/cortex#110 · the-metafactory/soma · visual harmonised with docs/diagrams/cortex-architecture.jpg
